name: drift-detection scan emits findings as one JSON object per line so downstream tooling can stream-parse the report
command:
mkdir -p src && printf 'a // rubric: ignore
b // rubric: ignore
' > src/two.js && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills/drift-detection/scan.sh" --paths src --out report.jsonl 2>err.txt; rc=$?; node -e 'const fs=require("fs");const lines=fs.readFileSync("report.jsonl","utf8").split("
").filter(Boolean);for(const l of lines){JSON.parse(l);}process.stdout.write(`lines=${lines.length}`)' > out.txt && grep -q 'lines=1' out.txt && echo ok 1>&2
